name: atdd-toolkit
description: Spec-first Acceptance Test-Driven Development for any language or framework. @atdd-cycle, @spec-writer, @spec-reviewer — no project setup required.

chatParticipants.name: atdd-cycle
chatParticipants.description: Run the full spec-first ATDD cycle from requirements to PR — analyze, spec, test, implement, quality gates, refactor, sync, PR

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze the project and write docs/project-profile.md

commands.name: spec
commands.description: Write a Gherkin feature file + technical spec from requirements

commands.name: tests
commands.description: Generate failing acceptance test stubs from the spec

commands.name: implement
commands.description: Implement production code to make the failing tests pass

commands.name: gates
commands.description: Run quality gates — lint, format, typecheck, build, test

commands.name: refactor
commands.description: Safely refactor with all tests green

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Spec & Doc Sync hard gate — verifies and repairs drift in-place

commands.name: pr
commands.description: Create a pull request for the completed feature

commands.name: review
commands.description: Address PR review comments using the spec change protocol

chatParticipants.name: full-autonomous-cycle
chatParticipants.description: Fully autonomous ATDD cycle — one human gate (spec approval), then hands-off through PR creation, Copilot review request, and comment resolution

chatParticipants.name: spec-writer
chatParticipants.description: Write Gherkin .feature files and paired technical specs from requirements or user stories

chatParticipants.name: spec-reviewer
chatParticipants.description: Review spec compliance, identify implementation drift, validate scenario coverage, and check README/profile alignment
